items.title: Kimi cuts coding token costs
items.oneliner: Moonshot's 1T-parameter Kimi K2.7-Code claims 30% fewer tokens, making long-horizon coding cheaper to run.

items.title: MaxProof pushes math agents forward
items.oneliner: MaxProof combines generation, verification, and repair, showing math agents are becoming systems, not single-shot models.

items.title: Anthropic is retreating on Fable guardrails
items.oneliner: Anthropic apologized after Fable silently downgraded AI research queries, proving hidden safety layers now create product risk.

items.title: AUR suffered a major supply-chain breach
items.oneliner: More than 400 AUR packages were reportedly backdoored with an infostealer and rootkit, a brutal open-source trust failure.

items.title: AMD left an updater RCE exposed
items.oneliner: AMD's AutoUpdate fetched executables over HTTP, turning software updates into a man-in-the-middle remote code execution path.

items.title: Canada faces encryption backlash
items.oneliner: Canada's Bill C-22 would force year-long metadata retention, reminding builders that privacy regulation can still weaken security.

items.title: An AI agent burned real cloud money
items.oneliner: One autonomous DN42 scanning attempt reportedly triggered a $6,500 AWS bill, which is the real cost of sloppy agents.

items.title: Zed is rethinking version control
items.oneliner: Zed's DeltaDB tracks every edit and conversation, betting agent-heavy coding needs something finer-grained than Git commits.

items.title: Homebrew added stronger trust defaults
items.oneliner: Homebrew 6.0 adds tap trust prompts and Linux sandboxing, a practical reminder that package managers are security products.

items.title: Apple broke Asahi Linux booting
items.oneliner: macOS 27 beta broke Asahi Linux boot paths again, underscoring how fragile alternative OS support remains on Apple Silicon.
